description: Deep recursion must trip the engine stack limit IDENTICALLY: a catchable RangeError, normalized to its name.

tolerance.notes: NORMALIZED: the exact stack depth at which recursion overflows is engine-specific, so a conformant applet must not branch on a depth count. The portable contract is that unbounded recursion throws a CATCHABLE RangeError (never a host crash) — the applet observes only error.name ('RangeError'), not the depth or the engine message. The runtime caps the C stack so the overflow is a JS RangeError, not an FFI crash.
